message:
fix(sink): prevent duplicate delivery on flush error after staging

BufferedSink::poll_send was returning the flush error to the caller
after the symbol was already accepted into the local buffer. The caller
would retry, pushing a second copy. Now returns Ok(()) after staging
since the symbol is committed — the flush error surfaces on the next
poll_flush or poll_send via poll_inner_terminal_error.

Audit batch 476 (9 files, ~14,655 lines):
- io_driver.rs (2136L) SOUND — split-lock poll, ABA prevention
- time/wheel.rs (2192L) SOUND — generation cancellation, bitmap skip
- transport/sink.rs (1876L) FIXED — duplicate delivery on flush error
- bracket.rs (1483L) SOUND — phase transitions correct
- reconnect.rs (1310L) SOUND — epoch-based stale prevention
- intrusive_wheel.rs (1322L) SOUND — no list corruption
- concurrency_limit.rs (1193L) SOUND — no permit leaks
- rate_limit.rs (1270L) SOUND — refill arithmetic verified
- blocking_pool.rs (1873L) SOUND — stranded-work handoff

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
